pages.raw_text:
r. B. Miller, general freight agent
Of the o. r. l. n, and his assistant,
II. Nj. Adams, have returned to Port
land from their trip to Spokane. MIR.
Adams is quoted as saying:


1T was not for conference with
the shippers that Mr.. Miller and I
went to Spokane. Other lines are rep
resented in that territory all the time
in addition to the o. r. 4 N. We saw
some of the shippers and there was
much routine business to be trans
acted, but the meeting was not in the
nature of a conference"


S. G. Fulton, assistant general freight
agent of the Northern Pacific, when
asked concerning the situation, is quot
ed thus by the Journal


"There has been no especial change
as yet. We think the time Of con-
ference is near, and that the present
situation, is an unnatural one, but no
definite time or place of meeting with
the shippers has been agreed on. 1t 10
probable that conference of all thu
interested roads will be held"
